summary: @across-toolkit/typescript-config postinstall exfiltrates installer credentials and environment to webhook.site
details: On npm install, postinstall.js and eslint-config/postinstall.js read installer credential files (~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.npmrc, GCP application default credentials), query cloud instance-metadata services at 169.254.169.254 (AWS IMDS) and http://metadata.google.internal (GCP), collect host identifiers via os.hostname() and child_process execSync of whoami/hostname, base64-encode the entire process.env, and POST the aggregated payload via https.request to a hardcoded webhook.site endpoint (https://webhook.site/a585f4ec-20f7-4bd1-bac7-f3e53799dc5f). The package name resembles the across-protocol namespace, consistent with typosquat targeting of CI and developer environments where NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, and cloud credentials are typically present.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
